none.justification: Correctly says no synchronization is present in eloop.c and gives solid file-level evidence: grep/include checks plus plain dl_list and socket-table operations. It misses the GT-required repo-wide pthread_create/pthread_* absence check, so not full credit.

ccodegraph.justification: Correctly rejects locks/mutex/RCU and supports it with source/grep evidence, including unsynchronized dl_list/socket tables and deferred signal handling. It does not verify the broader codebase for pthread_create/pthread_* symbols, which the rubric requires for a 3.

lsp.justification: Correctly identifies no lock/mutex/RCU/atomic primitives and explains the single-threaded signal-flag/main-loop model with relevant eloop.c details. It asserts no worker thread will concurrently access eloop state but does not substantiate that with the required repo-wide pthread_create/pthread_* absence check.
